copy.caption: A pig-nosed, rabbit-eared, digging machine built for one job: ants.
copy.shortDescription: The aardvark has a pig-like snout, long rabbit-like ears, and a body that looks like it couldn't quite decide what it wanted to be — but it is perfectly designed for digging into rock-hard termite mounds and slurping up thousands of insects a night. Its sticky tongue is up to thirty centimeters long and moves in and out of its mouth up to a hundred times a minute. Aardvarks are so important to the ecosystem that other animals move into their abandoned burrows — warthogs, hyenas, foxes, and owls all use old aardvark holes. Fun fact: The aardvark is the only living member of its entire order — there is nothing quite like it anywhere else on Earth.
